marketState: neutral, bearish fundamentals vs a bullish weather-demand spike
summary: Front price ($2.940) sits almost exactly at modeled fair value now (+$0.023 spread, status FAIR), so there is no near-term mispricing edge, but the fair-value curve rises to $3.128 by +21D and front is already SLIGHTLY UNDER that mark. Seasonality is a headwind: price is -13.7% YTD vs a -3.4% median (-10.3pp), the typical 4-week move is negative (-3.1%), and reliability is low (50pp spread). The standout signal is weather beyond the 10-day window - the 8-16 day demand outlook is +50.1% vs the 5-year norm and above the historical max range, with the demand index up +36% vs normal and accelerating (+24.5 in 24h) - a genuine medium-term bullish catalyst. Storage is a bearish counterweight (+184 Bcf vs 5y surplus, a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise), and technicals show a bearish trend/death-cross (50<200 EMA) on nearly every timeframe, though 4h RSI (24.9) and the 1d close below its lower Bollinger band look oversold. Net-net: near-term (1-10 day) signals are conflicting and offer no edge, while the medium-term picture leans cautiously constructive on the extreme heat-demand outlook and a rising fair-value path.

longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D weather signal is extreme (above the 5y max range, +50.1% vs normal, still accelerating) and the fair-value model's own curve climbs to $3.128 by +21D with front now SLIGHTLY UNDER that mark, consistent with rising fundamental fair value into peak summer. COT shows managed money net short at only the 35th percentile of the 5y range (not an extreme, contrarian-supportive level), though storage's +184 Bcf surplus and weak, low-reliability seasonality (below-median YTD price, negative typical 4w move) argue for small size and low conviction.
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close back below $2.75, the 8-16D demand anomaly fading back inside its normal 5y range, or a larger-than-expected storage build would invalidate this thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Price is essentially at fair value right now ($2.940 vs $2.917 fair, +$0.023 spread, FAIR), leaving no near-term mispricing edge, while signals conflict: near-term weather (1-7D demand +18.4% vs 5y, rangePos 0.99, not yet above max) is only modestly supportive, and technicals show a bearish trend/EMA structure (50<200) on 15m/1h/4h/1d/1w even as 4h RSI (24.9) and the 1d close below its lower Bollinger band flag an oversold condition. Futures curve data is unavailable in this snapshot, removing a key input for a clean short-term call.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a daily close above $3.05 (confirms near-term demand pulling price toward/through fair value, favors a tactical long) or a breakdown below $2.75 with confirming bearish MACD/RSI (favors a tactical short toward the lower fair-value band).
